notes:
MiniMax-M2.7-UD-IQ4_XS (101 GB GGUF) on a single DGX Spark with llama.cpp ngram-simple speculative decode at 108K context. Sherlock prose, pp=128, tg=128, c=1, depth=0. tokSOut = 30.88 t/s MEDIAN over n=38 warm trials across 2 independent rounds (mean 30.88, std 4.11, range 24.57-41.85). ttftMs = 807 median. Companion to my prior 32K submission (cmoi6yfxh000nk304si0uyyp5, 30.98 t/s c=32K) — same headline tg/s at 3.3× context window with provably stable long-context behavior.

What's new vs the 32K submission:
1) Recipe extended to c=108K with 5-phase stability battery + 10× back-to-back 100K torture (R10/R1 = 1.006, no drift).
2) Image base PINNED to nvcr.io/nvidia/cuda:13.0.0-devel-ubuntu24.04 — earlier CUDA 13.2/13.3 bases produced silent libcublas matmul corruption that quietly poisoned all longctx results until caught. Pre-flight: `readlink libcublas.so.13` must show 13.0.0.19.
3) Stability flags (3 that override defaults): --cache-reuse 256, --ctx-checkpoints 0, -cram 100. -cram=100 is intentional (NOT 4096 — verified 4096 collapses decode 5.6→1.0 t/s by run 4).

Spec acceptance ~36-82% across runs (server log statistics ngram_simple). peakVramGb=117 (free -g; 101 GB GGUF + ~16 GB KV in 128 GiB UMA, ~11 GiB headroom).

Full recipe + raw JSONs + Dockerfile: github.com/my-other-github-account/spark-bench-reproducers/tree/main/minimax-m27-longctx-108k-spark

By @banana_baeee.
